text: OSINTdefender: IRGC Wave 82 confirmed — two separate salvos of ballistic missiles fired at central Israel within 30 minutes. Tracking confirms Emad and Khorramshahr-4 airframes. 230 combined Axis of Resistance operations in prior 24h: 87 Hezbollah, 23 Iraqi Islamic Resistance, 120 IRGC. IDF Iron Dome/Arrow-3 working at near-capacity — saturation strategy is taxing interceptor stockpiles.
verificationNote: Confirmed via Times of Israel Day 27 liveblog and NewsFirst IRGC Wave 82 report.

text: Aurora Intel: IDF confirmed Sgt. Aviaad Volansky (21, 7th Armored Brigade) killed when Hezbollah anti-tank missile penetrated his Merkava tank in southern Lebanon. Trophy APS intercepted the first round; second penetrated. 4th IDF soldier killed in Lebanon. Earlier, Hezbollah commander Hassan Mohammad Bashir — anti-tank unit chief who orchestrated hundreds of AT missile attacks — killed in IDF drone strike in Hajir, southern Lebanon.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed Volansky killing. Bashir killing confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

text: CTP-ISW Day 27 assessment: Tangsiri's killing removes the principal architect of the Hormuz blockade, but the blockade infrastructure — mines, missile batteries, IRGC patrol vessels, vetting system — remains intact under new command. Short-term disruption to IRGC Navy coordination is likely, but blockade continues. CENTCOM has now struck 10,000+ Iranian targets. IDF accelerating strikes on arms factories (Mashhad, Tabriz airports) before potential ceasefire deadline.
verificationNote: CTP-ISW Day 27 assessment. CENTCOM 10,000 target milestone confirmed by Adm. Cooper statement.

text: Conflicts Watcher Day 27 PM summary: Trump Cabinet meeting major developments — 'Iran begging to make a deal'; Iran let 8-10 tankers through Hormuz as 'present'; Hegseth 'Iran has no navy, no navy leader'; WSJ Trump wants to 'wrap up conflict in coming weeks.' Iran Parliament Speaker warns any country aiding US Kharg Island seizure will face attacks on 'vital infrastructure.' Brent at $105.85 (+32.6% pre-war). UAE: 2 killed in Abu Dhabi by missile debris. Kuwait Hezbollah assassination cell (6 members) arrested.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNN, CNBC, WSJ, Al Jazeera Day 27 liveblog, Fortune oil price data.

text: Analysis: G7 meeting in France reveals deep US-Europe rift on Iran war. Germany's Pistorius called it 'a catastrophe for the world's economies.' Kallas: 'This is not Europe's war. We didn't start the war.' Rubio attending Day 2 of G7 to sell the war to skeptical allies. Pope Leo (March 24, Castel Gandolfo): 'I want to renew my call for a ceasefire, to work toward peace, not through weapons, but through dialogue.' Pakistan PM confirms Islamabad is 'prepared to host negotiations' — mediating US-Iran indirect talks via message exchanges.
verificationNote: Confirmed via CNBC G7 piece, Vatican News Pope Leo statement, NPR Pakistan mediation article.

text: Trump extends energy-strike pause to April 6 via Truth Social — citing Iranian government request. Brent peaked at $109/bbl after Iran denied ongoing negotiations, then retreated to ~$108 on the announcement. WTI hit $94.48. S&P 500 fell 1.74% — worst single day of the war. Market read: no imminent ceasefire, talks still exist but extremely fragile.
verificationNote: Trump Truth Social post confirmed by NPR, Bloomberg. Oil prices confirmed by Al Jazeera, NBC News markets.

text: BOMBSHELL at G7: EU foreign policy chief Kallas — "Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is supporting Iran with drones." UK Defense Secretary confirmed this independently via British intelligence. US envoy Witkoff contradicted allies saying Russia denied it and "we can take them at their word" — deepest US-EU intelligence rift of the conflict.
verificationNote: Kallas statement confirmed by CBS News, Bloomberg, Euronews. UK confirmation from Healey cited by Bloomberg. Witkoff divergence from White House press pool.

text: Pakistan FM Dar on-record first-ever confirmation: "US-Iran indirect talks are taking place through messages being relayed by Pakistan." Iran delivered 5 counter-conditions to the US 15-point plan: (1) complete end to all strikes, (2) no future war guarantees, (3) war reparations, (4) all fronts ceasefire including Hezbollah/Houthis, and (5) recognition of Iranian sovereignty over Strait of Hormuz. These are non-starters for the US.
verificationNote: Pakistan FM on-record confirmation per OPB/NPR, Times of Israel liveblog. Iran 5 conditions confirmed by OPB, Al Jazeera.

text: Day 27 Hormuz selective blockade update: Spain becomes first European nation added to Iran's approved transit list after Madrid banned US forces from using bases at Rota and Morón for Iran strikes and condemned the war. Malaysia also cleared. Full approved list: China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, Spain. ~2 AIS transits detected today. Iran Parliament drafting formal Hormuz toll law — $2M per ship per transit fee being formalized.
verificationNote: Spain passage confirmed by Euronews, US News. Malaysia confirmed by Brunei News Net. Parliament toll law from Al Jazeera Iran toll booth reporting.

text: Day 27 PM assessment: Trump energy pause extended to April 6 creates a 10-day window for diplomacy. IRGC-Hezbollah "joint and integrated" operation tonight signals escalating coordination across fronts. Iran fired cluster-warhead missiles at Kafr Qasim and Ganei Tikva (at least 11 impact sites confirmed). 60-jet IAF wave dropped 150+ bombs targeting Parchin, Isfahan, Mashhad, Tabriz, Shiraz, Bandar Abbas. Tangsiri killing disrupts IRGC Navy short-term but blockade infrastructure fully intact.
verificationNote: Joint IRGC-Hezbollah operation confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. Cluster munitions confirmed by Times of Israel. IAF 60-jet wave confirmed by Alma Research 18:00 report.

text: Sen. Murkowski (R-AK) announced she is drafting a formal AUMF for the Iran war — says Trump admin won't answer closed-door briefing questions about war scope or end goals. House Democrats delaying their war powers vote until mid-April. Senate defeated third war powers resolution 53-47 on March 24. WSJ: Trump told aides to wrap up conflict "in coming weeks" — tied to China trip deadline May 14-15. War is becoming politically toxic: Trump approval 36%, war approval 35%, both at session lows.
verificationNote: Murkowski AUMF confirmed by Bloomberg. War powers vote timeline from Axios. Poll numbers from Reuters/Ipsos and Pew Research.

text: OSINTdefender: IRGC Wave 83 now confirmed — targeting simultaneously Al-Dhafra (UAE), Al-Udeiri (Saudi Arabia), Ali Al-Salem (Kuwait), and Sheikh Isa (Bahrain). First wave to hit all four major US Gulf bases simultaneously. Patriot maintenance hangar at Sheikh Isa specifically targeted. IRGC missiles contained inscriptions thanking Spain, Pakistan, India, and Germany — psychological ops component of escalating Hormuz diplomacy.
verificationNote: IRGC Wave 83 confirmed by Tribune India, ANI News, GlobalSecurity.org. Targeting pattern matches IRGC statement.

text: GeoConfirmed: IAF Yazd strike confirmed via open-source satellite imagery — Yazd naval missile/sea mine production facility shows multiple impact craters in maintenance and assembly halls. This site was the primary production hub for IRGC naval anti-ship missiles and mines. Elimination of this facility significantly degrades Iran's ability to replace Hormuz blockade mines and reload sea-launched missiles for weeks.
verificationNote: Yazd strike confirmed by Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel liveblog, The Media Line.

text: CTP-ISW Day 28 early assessment: IAF massive Tehran strike (60 jets, 150+ munitions) represents the largest single-city bombing run of the war. Multiple weapon manufacturing sites hit simultaneously — this is the '48-hour maximum damage' push Netanyahu ordered before potential ceasefire. IDF also struck Kermanshah and Dezful (~20 sorties, 70 munitions) targeting ballistic missile launchers. 10,000 additional US troops including 82nd Airborne now reportedly being prepared — Kharg Island amphibious assault remains the highest-probability escalation scenario.
verificationNote: Tehran strike confirmed by Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel. Kermanshah/Dezful confirmed by Times of Israel. Troops confirmed by Axios, Stars and Stripes.

text: Day 28 early market summary: Brent .99/bbl (+4.1% from .74 close), WTI .48/bbl (+4.6% from .31 close). Oil surge driven by Iran's firm rejection of US 15-point peace plan — markets re-pricing for extended conflict. AAA national gas avg .981/gal — /bin/zsh.019 from .00 milestone. S&P 500 fell 1.74% on March 26 (worst day of war). Goldman Sachs: -20/bbl geopolitical risk premium embedded in prices.
verificationNote: Brent .99 confirmed by NBC News. WTI .48 confirmed by FX Leaders. Gas .981 confirmed by AAA. Goldman Sachs risk premium from CNBC.

text: Aurora Intel: Rubio-G7 fallout deeper than reported. France's defense chief said allies were not informed before US-Israel launched operations. Germany's Pistorius called it a catastrophe. 35 nations joined France-hosted Hormuz reopening military conference. Key detail: Kallas not only revealed Russia intel-sharing but specifically said Russia is providing satellite imagery and upgrading Iranian drones targeting American warships. This is Russia's most direct military involvement with Iran since war began.
verificationNote: G7 dynamics confirmed by Bloomberg, CNBC, NPR, Washington Post. Russia drone/satellite detail from Bloomberg Kallas statement.

text: Pakistan's diplomatic position is increasingly complex: FM Dar confirms indirect US-Iran talks via Pakistan, but simultaneously Iran fired Wave 83 missiles carrying messages thanking Pakistan for solidarity. Pakistan is threading an extremely narrow needle — trusted by Iran as a friendly mediator while also carrying messages for the US. Pakistan PM separately stated Islamabad is prepared to host direct talks in Islamabad if both sides agree. Window for direct Araghchi-Witkoff meeting still open per Axios sources.
verificationNote: Pakistan FM Dar on-record confirmed by OPB/NPR. Islamabad hosting offer confirmed by Bloomberg, Al Jazeera.
